sections.heading: Helpful resources
added: Embassy of Korea visa information Korea Immigration Service (KIS)

sections.heading: Visas
added: You can stay in South Korea without a visa for 90 days for tourism or business.

Starting January 1, 2027, you will need a Korean Electronic Travel Authorization (K-ETA) before traveling to South Korea There is an exemption to the K-ETA requirement in effect through December 31, 2026.
You must have a valid passport to enter South Korea.
There is no specific expiration date requirement.
A visa is required for all other travel purposes, including employment, teaching English, and stays longer than 90 days.

sections.heading: Extending visas
added: In the event of an overstay: Apply for a visa extension from the Korea Immigration Service (KIS) before attempting to leave the country.
Consult with KIS regarding changes in visa category.
Important: Exceeding your authorized stay or not having a valid visa may result in an exit ban, detention, and/or fines.

sections.heading: Exit permits
added: South Korean immigration authorities can stop you from leaving South Korea if you break immigration or criminal laws.
If you are facing an exit ban, consider hiring a local South Korean lawyer to help you resolve the problem.
Minors usually don’t need an exit permit.
However, if one parent requests a travel restriction on their child, South Korean authorities might prevent the child from leaving, even with the other parent.

sections.heading: Military personnel or DOD on orders (including family members)
added: Consult DOD Foreign Clearance Guide and follow all instructions.
Enter South Korea with DOD identification and travel orders.
Family members and dependents of military personnel on DOD orders must present a valid official or regular passport.
Do not transit other countries such as China without a passport and appropriate visas.
Be sure to clear customs when you arrive, so you won't have problems when you leave.

sections.heading: U.S. Government Executive Branch personnel on official business and DOD personnel assigned to the U.S. Embassy (including family members)
added: Employees assigned to Mission South Korea should enter South Korea with a diplomatic or official passport and a diplomatic or official Korean visa obtained through their sponsoring agency.
Check with your sponsoring agency about other requirements.
Through December 31, 2025, TDY visitors traveling to South Korea for up to 90 days on diplomatic or official passports do not require South Korean visas and do not require a K-ETA.
TDY visitors must obtain country clearance from Department of State eCC system or DOD APACS system.

sections.heading: HIV and AIDS restriction
added: The Department of State is not aware of any HIV and AIDS entry restrictions for visitors or foreign residents in South Korea.

sections.heading: Advisory summary
added: Norway is generally a safe destination for travelers.
Be aware of landslides and avalanches.
Norway’s steep mountains make these common.
Heavy summer rain or winter snow accumulation can cause them.
A special kind of clay called “quick clay” (kvikkleire) can cause ground to collapse in some areas where people live.

sections.heading: If you decide to travel to Norway:
added: Enroll in the Smart Traveler Enrollment Program (STEP) to get important updates and alerts from the U.S.
embassy or consulate contact you or your emergency contact in an emergency.
Review the Country Security Report for Oslo.
Visit the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) website for the latest Travel Health Information for Norway.
Whether you’re a first time or frequent traveler, use the International Travel Checklist.

sections.heading: About Norway
added: Norway is located in Europe, in the Northern Hemisphere.
It has maritime borders with the North Sea, Norwegian Sea, and Barents Sea.
It also shares land borders with Sweden, Finland, and Russia.
Norway is slightly larger than New Mexico.
There is 1 time zone, Central European Time (CET).

sections.heading: Valid passport requirements
added: Must have 6 month’s validity beyond your arrival date.
Your passport must have at least 2 blank pages.

sections.heading: Currency on entry and exit
added: Entry maximum: 25,000 Norwegian Kroner (NOK) Exit maximum: 25,000 Norwegian Kroner (NOK)

sections.heading: Tips from the U.S. embassy
added: Most cities ban drinking alcohol in the street, except at registered cafes and bars.
It is illegal to bring in marijuana, cannabis, or CBD products or derivatives.
Fines range from a few hundred to several thousand Norwegian kroner.
You may also face arrest or detention.
It is illegal to bring in guns, ammunition, and even spent shells or casings without the proper permits and documentation.

sections.heading: Help for U.S. citizens
added: Embassy Oslo Morgedalsvegen 36, 0378 Oslo, Norway Hours: 08:30 - 17:00 (by appointment for consular services) Mailing address: PO Box 4075 AMB, 0244 Oslo, Norway Main telephone: +47-2130-8540 Emergency after-hours: +47-2130-8540 Email: osloacs@state.gov Websit

über den Flughafen in Valencia (VLN) abgewickelt, der hierfür aufgrund seiner Größe nicht ausgelegt ist.

sections.heading: Incendie dans la zone de Los Gallardos (province d'Almeria, Andalousie)
added: Un incendie s’est déclaré le 9 juillet sur la commune de Los Gallardos et continue à se propager rapidement en raison de la sécheresse et du vent.
On déplore déjà plusieurs victimes et de nombreuses personnes blessées ou disparues.
Actuellement, 8 communes sont concernées par cet incendie et 8000 personnes ont déjà été évacuées.
Il convient de ne pas traverser cette région (plusieurs routes sont déjà fermées) et de se conformer strictement aux instructions de sécurité des autorités locales.

Il est possible de se renseigner sur la situation et les recommandations des autorités en suivant : @Plan_INFOCA (compte du service de lutte contre les incendies en zones forestières et rurales) @112Andalucia (compte des services d'assistance sanitaire et d'ur

sections.heading: Before you travel
added: No travel can be guaranteed safe.
Read all the advice in this guide.

You may also find it helpful to: see general advice for women travellers read our guide on disability and travel abroad see general advice for LGBT+ travellers read about safety for solo and independent travel see advice on volunteering and adventure travel ab

sections.heading: Travel insurance
added: If you choose to travel, research your destinations and get appropriate travel insurance.
Insurance should cover your itinerary, planned activities and expenses in an emergency.

sections.heading: About FCDO travel advice
added: The Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) provides advice about risks of travel to help you make informed decisions.
Find out more about FCDO travel advice.

sections.heading: Get travel advice updates
added: Sign up to get email notifications when this advice is updated.
Follow FCDO: travel advice on X travel advice on Facebook Travel Aware on Instagram
Sign up to get email notifications when this travel advice is updated.

sections.heading: Entry requirements
added: This information is for people travelling on a full ‘British citizen’ passport from the UK.
It is based on the UK government’s understanding of the current rules for the most common types of travel.
The authorities in Czechia set and enforce entry rules.
If you’re not sure how these requirements apply to you, contact the Czech Embassy in the UK.

sections.heading: Passport validity requirements
added: Czechia follows Schengen area rules.

Your passport must: have a ‘date of issue’ less than 10 years before the date you arrive – if you renewed your passport before 1 October 2018, it might have a date of issue that is more than 10 years ago, making it invalid for entry to the Schengen area have a
Renew your passport if you need to.
You will be denied entry if you do not have a valid travel document or try to use a passport that has been reported lost or stolen.

sections.heading: Dual nationals
added: When returning to the UK, British citizens must carry a valid British passport or certificate of entitlement.

sections.heading: Visa requirements
added: You can travel without a visa to the Schengen area, which includes Czechia, for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.

This applies if you travel: as a tourist to visit family or friends to attend business meetings, cultural or sports events for short-term studies or training The requirements for working in Czechia are different.
If you’re travelling to other Schengen countries as well, make sure your whole visit is within the 90-day limit.
Visits to Schengen countries in the 180 days before you travel count towards your 90 days.
If you overstay the 90-day visa-free limit, you may be banned from entering Schengen countries for up to 3 years.

changes.updateNote: Updated information about regional tensions and conflict affecting Israel (‘Warnings and insurance’ page).

sections.heading: Warnings and insurance
added: Your travel insurance could be invalidated if you travel against advice from the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO).

sections.heading: Gaza
added: FCDO advises against all travel to Gaza
FCDO advises against all travel to Gaza (including the waters off Gaza).
You should not approach the perimeter fence surrounding the Gaza Strip.
FCDO cannot offer routine consular assistance in Gaza.
If you travel to Gaza against our advice, review your security arrangements regularly.

sections.heading: Areas near Gaza
added: FCDO advises against all travel within 500m of the border with Gaza
There have been repeated instances of rocket fire from Gaza, which may reach well beyond the Gaza border area.
Israel has often responded with air strikes into Gaza.

You should: make sure you know the safety actions to take if an Israeli warning siren sounds – no warning sirens will sound inside Gaza follow advice from local authorities stay informed of the security situation through the media

sections.heading: The West Bank
added: FCDO advises against all travel to: Tulkarm Governorate, Jenin Governorate, and Tubas Governorate except Route 90

sections.heading: Northern Israel and Occupied Golan Heights
added: FCDO advises against all travel to: Areas north of Route 89 between Nahariyya and Dalton; north of a straight eastward line from Dalton to Route 91; and north of Route 91 across the Golan Heights, excluding routes 89 and 91 themselves Within 500m of the Area o
Do not attempt to enter the occupied Golan Heights through Syria Sheba’a Farms and Ghajja

sections.heading: West Bank
added: FCDO advises against all but essential travel to: The rest of the West Bank, except for: East Jerusalem, Bethlehem Governate, Ramallah Municipality, Jericho municipality and all areas which lie south of it (this includes the Allenby crossing).

Highway 1 that links Jerusalem and Tel Aviv and the areas that lie to the south and west of the Highway (this includes Highway 3), and Route 443 and Highway 45 which link Jerusalem and Tel Aviv Find out more about why FCDO advises against travel.
There has been a heightened state of tension and violent clashes resulting in deaths in the West Bank since the outbreak of conflict in October 2023.
Expect road closures and numerous checkpoints across the West Bank.
There are no warning sirens or official air raid shelters inside the West Bank, outside of Israeli settlements.

sections.heading: Regional tensions
added: The US and Iran have announced a memorandum of understanding in relation to the conflict in the Middle East.
The situation remains unpredictable and attacks could resume at short notice.
Should hostilities resume, British nationals should: read If you’re affected by a crisis abroad - GOV.UK.

This includes guidance on “how to prepare for a crisis” with suggestions on what you might include in your emergency supplies and “what to do in a crisis” follow the instructions of the local authorities Visit Israel’s Home Front Command sign up to FCDO Travel
An interior stairwell or a room with as few external walls or windows as possible may provide additional protection.

sections.heading: Shelter in place
added: Follow the instructions of the local authorities.
If you are advised to take shelter, Israel’s Home Front Command provides guidance on how to find a protected space.
The best option is a residential protected space (Mamad, Mamak or Maman) or a communal shelter.
If that is not possible, choose a stairwell without windows and exterior walls and stay at its centre.
Sign up to receive information and alerts.

sections.heading: Aktuelles
added: Der Taifun „Bavi“ wird voraussichtlich im Verlauf des 11.
Juli 2026 in China auf Land treffen.
Vorhersagen zufolge werden die Auswirkungen des Sturms besonders die südostchinesischen Provinzen Shanghai, Anhui, Fujian, Zhejiang, Jiangsu und Guangdong treffen.
Es ist mit starken Niederschlägen, sehr hohen Windgeschwindigkeiten und starkem Wellengang zu rechnen.
Des Weiteren besteht Gefahr durch herabfallende Äste und umstürzende Bäume; in Hanglagen kann es zu Erdrutschen kommen.

Your travel plans may be affected, even if your destination is not in the Middle East.

sections.heading: Travel insurance

Insurance should cover your itinerary, planned activities and expenses in an emergency.

sections.heading: Entry requirements
added: This information is for people travelling on a full ‘British citizen’ passport from the UK.
It is based on the UK government’s understanding of the Federated States of Micronesia’s current rules for the most common types of travel.
The authorities in the Federated States of Micronesia set and enforce entry rules.
If you’re not sure how these requirements apply to you, contact the Federated States of Micronesia Immigration Office.
You can also check the Federated States of Micronesia’s entry requirements on its tourism website.

sections.heading: Passport validity requirements
added: To enter the Federated States of Micronesia, your passport must have an ‘expiry date’ at least 120 days after the date you arrive.
You will need to clear immigration even for a domestic flight, so you will always need your passport when you fly in the FSM.
Check with your travel provider that your passport and other travel documents meet requirements.
Renew your passport if you need to.
You will be denied entry if you do not have a valid travel document or try to use a passport that has been reported lost or stolen.

sections.heading: Dual nationals
added: When returning to the UK, British citizens must carry a valid British passport or certificate of entitlement.

sections.heading: Entry requirements
added: This information is for people travelling on a full ‘British citizen’ passport from the UK.
It is based on the UK government’s understanding of Eswatini’s current rules for the most common types of travel.
The authorities in Eswatini set and enforce entry rules.
If you’re not sure how these requirements apply to you, contact the Eswatini High Commission in the UK.

sections.heading: Passport validity requirements
added: To enter Eswatini, your passport must have an ‘expiry date’ at least 3 months after the date you arrive and at least 2 blank pages for entry stamps.
If you’re also travelling to South Africa, your passport must have 2 additional blank pages.
Check with your travel provider that your passport and other travel documents meet requirements.
Renew your passport if you need to.
You will be denied entry if you do not have a valid travel document or try to use a passport that has been reported lost or stolen.

sections.heading: Dual nationals
added: When returning to the UK, British citizens must carry a valid British passport or certificate of entitlement.

sections.heading: Visa requirements
added: You can visit Eswatini without a visa for up to 30 days.
You can apply for a 30-day extension from the Ministry of Home Affairs.
If you want to stay longer than 60 days, you must apply for a temporary residence permit.
Make sure you get your passport stamped.
Check the stamp in your passport has the correct date.

sections.heading: Information and support
added: If you are concerned about someone in the area, call this helpline +34 677 904 624 provided by the Psychological Intervention Group for Emergencies and Disasters (GIPED).
English speaking staff are available.
If you or a family member have been affected and need consular assistance, call +34 917 146 300.
If you are in the UK, call +44 (0)20 7008 5000.

sections.heading: Typhon Bavi - appel à la vigilance
added: L’agence météorologique japonaise (JMA) a émis une alerte à l’approche du typhon BAVI ce jeudi 9 juillet.
Le typhon BAVI traversera des îles d’Okinawa et des îles d’Amami ce vendredi 10 et samedi 11 juillet.
De très fortes précipitations orageuses, des vents violents et de très hautes vagues sont attendues sur ces territoires.

Il convient de faire preuve de prudence, de se tenir informé et de suivre les consignes des autorités locales : Sur l’évolution du typhon BAVI : https://www.jma.go.jp/bosai/map.html#5/25.785/124.844/&elem=root&typhoon=all&contents=typhoon&lang=en Sur les alert
addedNl: Het Japan Meteorological Agency (JMA) heeft donderdag 9 juli een waarschuwing afgegeven voor de nadering van tyfoon BAVI.
Tyfoon BAVI trekt aanstaande vrijdag 10 juli en zaterdag 11 juli over de Okinawa-eilanden en de Amami-eilanden.
In deze gebieden worden zeer zware stormachtige neerslag, harde wind en zeer hoge golven verwacht.

Het is belangrijk om voorzichtig te zijn, op de hoogte te blijven en de instructies van de lokale autoriteiten op te volgen: Over de evolutie van Typhoon BAVI: https://www.jma.go.jp/bosai/map.html#5/25.785/124.844/&elem=root&typhoon=all&contents=typhoon&lang=en Over waarschuwingen

sections.heading: Travellers under 18
added: If you’re entering Mozambique with a child under the age of 18, you will need to present an unabridged birth certificate listing both parents.
Where the child is travelling with one parent, or without either parent, a notarised parental consent affidavit (not older than three months) is required.

changes.description: Advies bijgewerkt door de bron (2026-06-08 → 2026-07-09).
changes.updateNote: Addition of information about extreme heat (see ‘Extreme weather and natural disasters' on the ‘Safety and security' page).

sections.heading: Extreme Heat
added: Japanese summers are extremely hot and humid, with temperatures often exceeding 35 degrees in July and August.
High humidity often makes it feel much hotter.
As a result, heat stroke and heat-related illnesses pose a significant risk during the summer months.
The Japanese Environmental Agency publishes a heat stroke index, that identifies areas have a heat stroke alert currently in effect.
The website also provides information about heat stroke, its symptoms, and practical advice on how to prevent heat-related illnesses.

sections.heading: Visa requirements
added: Once you’re in Tajikistan, you can apply for a visa extension at the Consular Department of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
Most visa categories can be extended in Tajikistan.
However, Tourist Visas (T visas) cannot be extended.
If you are travelling on a Tourist Visa, you must leave the country before your visa expires.
For further information on visa requirements and extensions, visit Consular Department of the MFA.

sections.heading: Sirènes d’alerte suite aux survols de l’espace aérien jordanien
added: Des sirènes d’alerte ont retenti en Jordanie le 9 juillet suite aux attaques de missiles ou drones iraniens.

Dans ce contexte, il est important de demeurer attentif aux sirènes d’alerte et de se mettre à l’abri dans une salle close, sans ouverture ni fenêtre, jusqu’à la diffusion de l’alarme de fin de danger.
En cas de chute de débris, il convient de s’éloigner immédiatement du lieu de l’incident.

Plus généralement, il convient de demeurer vigilant, en particulier de : rester joignable par téléphone et SMS et consulter ses e-mails régulièrement ; consulter régulièrement le site internet de l’ambassade de France (https://jo.ambafrance.org/) et ses compte
L’espace aérien jordanien est ouvert et fonctionne normalement.
addedNl: Op 9 juli klonken waarschuwingssirenes in Jordanië na Iraanse raket- of drone-aanvallen.

In deze context is het belangrijk alert te blijven op waarschuwingssirenes en te schuilen in een afgesloten ruimte, zonder openingen of ramen, totdat het alarm voor het einde van het gevaar wordt uitgezonden.
Bij vallend puin dient u onmiddellijk de plaats van het incident te verlaten.

Meer in het algemeen moet u waakzaam blijven, met name: bereikbaar blijven via telefoon en sms en uw e-mails regelmatig controleren; raadpleeg regelmatig de website van de Franse ambassade (https://jo.ambafrance.org/) en haar rekeningen
Het Jordaanse luchtruim is open en functioneert normaal.

changes.description: Advies bijgewerkt door de bron (2026-07-03 → 2026-07-09).
changes.updateNote: New information about attending festivals and concerts and using swimming pools and updated information about protecting yourself and your belongings, and beach safety (‘Safety and security’ page).

sections.heading: Staying longer than 90 days in a 180-day period
added: This does not apply to you if you’re the holder of a residence permit or long stay visa.

sections.heading: Protecting yourself and your belongings
added: Be alert to the risk of street crime.
Take care on public transport and at crowded bus and tram stops.
Thieves use distraction techniques and often work in teams.
Look after your passport, money and personal belongings when travelling, checking in luggage at the airport or arranging car hire.
Do not carry all your valuables in one place.

sections.heading: Attacks and sexual assault
added: Attacks including sexual assaults, are rare but do occur.
The risk is highest late at night around popular nightlife locations.
In an emergency, call 112.
Avoid splitting up from your friends, do not go off with people you don’t know and don’t let a friend walk back to their accommodation alone.
Set up a group chat to keep in touch.

sections.heading: Drink spiking and alcohol
added: Be alert to the possible use of ‘date rape’ and other drugs including GHB and liquid ecstasy.
Buy your own drinks and always keep them in sight to avoid them being spiked.
Alcohol and drugs can reduce your vigilance, making you less in control.
If you drink, know your limit.
Drinks served in bars in Portugal are often stronger than those in the UK.

sections.heading: Local laws governing public behaviour in Albufeira
Festivals, concerts and cultural events
added: Many large events are held in Portugal each year.
Follow the advice of police and local authorities when attending events.
Take care of your valuables if attending a festival or large concert.
See the festival travel checklist for more advice.

sections.heading: Swimming pools
added: Always supervise children.
Keep small children within arms-reach in and around swimming pools, even if they can swim or there is a lifeguard present.
Be particularly vigilant of children if you are staying in a villa with a private pool.

changes.updateNote: New information about Electronic Overseas Citizen of India Card (e-OCI) and additional information about Ebola health screening and a  mandatory Self-Declaration Form for all international passengers arriving in India ('Entry requirements' page).

sections.heading: Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) cardholders
added: An Electronic Overseas Citizen of India (e-OCI) was launched in June 2026 and means holders of an e-OCI card need not carry a physical copy of the card.
More information is available at Online OCI Services.

sections.heading: Ebola
added: As a public health measure, India has introduced health screening measures, including completing a mandatory Self-Declaration Form for all international passengers arriving in India.
The Self-Declaration Form should be completed up to 24 hours before arrival through the Air Suvidha Portal.
If you don’t complete the form before you arrive, expect delays.
Monitor local media and follow the advice of the airlines you are travelling with, local authorities and your travel company.

We also advise do not travel to border areas with Gaza (Palestine), Lebanon and Syria. There remains a risk of military action. If this happens, prioritise your safety. Follow warnings from local authorit

sections.heading: Latest update
added: We also advise do not travel to border areas with Gaza (Palestine), Lebanon and Syria.
If warned of an imminent attack, move to an enclosed hardened shelter.
Military conflict in the region may cause travel disruptions including airspace closures and flight cancellations.

sections.heading: Safety
added: We continue to advise reconsider your need to travel due to the unpredictable security situation in Israel and the region.

sections.heading: Notas importantes
added: SE RECOMIENDA VIAJAR CON PRECAUCIÓN..
Lea con atención el resto de estas recomendaciones de viaje.
España dispone de Consulados Generales en Casablanca, Agadir, Nador, Larache, Rabat (que incluye Fez y Meknés), Tánger y Tetuán, además de Consulados Honorarios en Marrakech, Fez y Essaouira.

Puede obtener más información a este respecto en este enlace En casos de emergencia grave fuera del horario de oficina, puede llamar al teléfono de emergencia del Consulado en cuya circunscripción se encuentre.
Este teléfono es de uso exclusivo para ciudadanos españoles y está reservado para atender asuntos de especial gravedad (accidentes, desapariciones, peligro de muerte, etc.).
addedNl: HET WORDT AANBEVOLEN OM VOORZICHTIG TE REIZEN.
Lees de rest van deze reisadviezen aandachtig door.
Spanje heeft algemene consulaten in Casablanca, Agadir, Nador, Larache, Rabat (waartoe ook Fez en Meknes behoren), Tanger en Tetouan, evenals ereconsulaten in Marrakech, Fez en Essaouira.
Meer informatie hierover kunt u krijgen via deze link. Bij ernstige calamiteiten buiten kantooruren kunt u bellen met het noodtelefoonnummer van het consulaat in wiens kiesdistrict u zich bevindt.
Dit telefoonnummer is uitsluitend bestemd voor Spaans staatsburgers en is gereserveerd voor bijzonder ernstige zaken (ongevallen, verdwijningen, levensgevaar, enz.).

sections.heading: Documentación y visados
added: Pasaporte: Para entrar en Marruecos, se exige pasaporte en vigor con un plazo de validez superior a los 6 (seis) meses y que deberá ser válido durante toda la estancia en el país.
Antes de iniciar el viaje se recomienda consultar a su centro de vacunación internacional, así como leer las recomendaciones sanitarias por países del Ministerio de Sanidad en su página web .
addedNl: Paspoort: Om Marokko binnen te komen is een geldig paspoort met een geldigheidsduur van meer dan 6 (zes) maanden vereist en moet tijdens uw verblijf in het land geldig zijn.

Voordat u aan de reis begint, wordt aanbevolen om uw internationale vaccinatiecentrum te raadplegen en de gezondheidsaanbevelingen per land van het Ministerie van Volksgezondheid op haar website te lezen.

sections.heading: Seguridad
added: No obstante, a la luz de la situación internacional actual y, en particular, de la inestabilidad en la región vecina del Sahel, no se puede descartar la posibilidad de algún tipo de agresión terrorista o ataque violento contra intereses y nacionales occidental
En éstas, las zonas cercanas a la frontera con Argelia (sin delimitación visible en su gran mayoría y por ello propensas a ser cruzadas sin advertirlo) pueden resultar especialmente problemáticas.
Zonas de riesgo medio: Es conveniente evitar viajar solo o en grupos pequeños por zonas rurales aisladas, pero en todo caso hacerlo en más de un vehículo y de preferencia todoterreno.

En la cadena montañosa del Rif, alrededor de la zona de Ketama, debe extremarse la precaución por la presencia de personas que, en ocasiones, intentan vender hachís insistentemente a los turistas extranjeros.
Igualmente se recomienda ser prudente respecto de posibles compañías accidentales de desconocidos y recurrir, en la medida de lo posible, a los servicios de guías oficiales registrados.
addedNl: In het licht van de huidige internationale situatie en in het bijzonder de instabiliteit in de aangrenzende Sahelregio kan de mogelijkheid van een vorm van terroristische agressie of gewelddadige aanval op westerse belangen en onderdanen echter niet worden uitgesloten.

In deze gebieden kunnen vooral de gebieden dicht bij de grens met Algerije (de overgrote meerderheid zonder zichtbare afbakening en daarom gemakkelijk zonder waarschuwing te worden overschreden) problematisch zijn.

Gebieden met gemiddeld risico: Het is raadzaam om niet alleen of in kleine groepen door geïsoleerde plattelandsgebieden te reizen, maar doe dit in ieder geval in meer dan één voertuig en bij voorkeur off-road.

In het Rifgebergte, rond het Ketama-gebied, moet uiterste voorzichtigheid in acht worden genomen vanwege de aanwezigheid van mensen die soms aandringend proberen hasj aan buitenlandse toeristen te verkopen.
Het wordt ook aanbevolen voorzichtig te zijn met mogelijk toevallig gezelschap van vreemden en, voor zover mogelijk, een beroep te doen op de diensten van officieel geregistreerde gidsen.

sections.heading: Sanidad
added: Las condiciones sanitarias en Marruecos son, en general, aceptables.
En las principales ciudades se puede recurrir, en caso de necesidad, a clínicas privadas donde hay buenos profesionales formados con frecuencia en España o Francia.
Sin embargo, en ciudades de menor población y, en general, en el medio rural, las instalaciones sanitarias son de un nivel muy inferior, poco dotadas y con personal auxiliar poco cualificado.

Conviene extremar las medidas de higiene, prestando atención a la comida (lavando bien las frutas por ejemplo), bebida (agua embotellada fuera de las grandes ciudades) y alojamiento; los puestos ambulantes donde se sirven comidas y aquellos situados al borde d
Para mayor información sobre cuestiones sanitarias, puede consultar la web del Ministerio de Sanidad y la protección social marroquí, así como la de la Organización Mundial de la salud.
addedNl: De sanitaire omstandigheden in Marokko zijn over het algemeen acceptabel.
In de grote steden kunt u, indien nodig, een beroep doen op privéklinieken waar goede professionals zijn die regelmatig worden opgeleid in Spanje of Frankrijk.

In steden met een kleinere bevolking en, in het algemeen, op het platteland, zijn de gezondheidsvoorzieningen echter van een veel lager niveau, slecht uitgerust en met slecht gekwalificeerd hulppersoneel.

Het is raadzaam om extreme hygiënemaatregelen te nemen, waarbij aandacht wordt besteed aan eten (fruit goed wassen bijvoorbeeld), drinken (flessenwater buiten de grote steden) en accommodatie; de straatstalletjes waar eten wordt geserveerd en de kraampjes aan de rand ervan
Voor meer informatie over gezondheidskwesties kunt u de website van het Marokkaanse ministerie van Volksgezondheid en Sociale Bescherming raadplegen, evenals die van de Wereldgezondheidsorganisatie.

sections.heading: Divisas
added: En la actualidad la cotización es de aproximadamente 1€= 10,80 dirhams, pudiendo oscilar el tipo de cambio en un +-5%.
El dirham no tiene convertibilidad exterior, estando sujeta su importación y exportación al control previo del “Office des Changes".

Respecto a la moneda local, los viajeros están autorizados a llevar consigo un valor no superior a los 2.000 dirhams, pues de lo contrario también deberán declararlo, pues la exportación de moneda local está prohibida.
addedNl: Momenteel bedraagt ​​de prijs ongeveer € 1 = 10,80 dirham, en de wisselkoers kan met +-5% schommelen.
De dirham heeft geen buitenlandse convertibiliteit; de import en export ervan zijn onderworpen aan voorafgaande controle door het “Office des Changes”.
Wat de lokale valuta betreft, mogen reizigers een waarde van niet meer dan 2.000 dirham bij zich dragen, anders moeten ze dit ook aangeven, aangezien de export van lokale valuta verboden is.

sections.heading: Otros
added: SEGURO DE VIAJE Es importante contratar, en origen, un seguro de viaje fiable que incluya asistencia y repatriación sanitaria.

ENTRADA DE VEHÍCULOS La entrada con vehículo en territorio marroquí queda reflejada en la ficha policial del interesado, por lo que es obligatorio salir siempre con el mismo vehículo con el que se entró en el país, salvo que pueda justificarse documentalmente

Si la entrada en Marruecos se hace utilizando un vehículo del que no se es propietario, es necesario estar provisto de un documento de cesión debidamente validado ante la Embajada de Marruecos en Madrid o ante alguno de los Consulados de Marruecos en España.
Si se entra en Marruecos con vehículo éste debe estar asegurado con la máxima cobertura posible.
Si se viaja con vehículo propio o de alquiler, es muy habitual pasar por controles de la Gendarmería Real en carretera.
addedNl: REISVERZEKERING Het is belangrijk om aan de bron een betrouwbare reisverzekering af te sluiten die medische hulp en repatriëring omvat.

BINNENKOMST VAN HET VOERTUIG Het binnenkomen met een voertuig op Marokkaans grondgebied wordt weerspiegeld in het politiedossier van de belanghebbende. Het is dus verplicht om altijd te vertrekken met hetzelfde voertuig waarmee u het land bent binnengekomen, tenzij dit kan worden gedocumenteerd.

Als u Marokko binnenkomt met een voertuig dat niet uw eigendom is, moet u over een overdrachtsdocument beschikken dat naar behoren is gevalideerd bij de Marokkaanse ambassade in Madrid of bij een van de Marokkaanse consulaten in Spanje.
Als u met een voertuig Marokko binnenkomt, moet deze verzekerd zijn met de maximaal mogelijke dekking.
Als u met uw eigen of huurauto reist, is het heel gebruikelijk om onderweg controleposten van de Royal Gendarmerie te passeren.

sections.heading: Embajada del Reino de Marruecos en España Serrano, 179 28002 Madrid Teléfono: 91 563 10 90 Fax: 91 561 78 87​
Sito en 29, Rue D'Alger, se encuentra en obras, únicamente permanece en esta dirección la sección de legalizaciones.

sections.heading: Interprovincial travel
added: An outbreak of Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD) is affecting parts of western and southern Mongolia.
FMD mainly affects animals, but control measures may disrupt your travel plans, especially in rural regions.

Mongolian authorities have introduced movement controls, quarantine measures and possible access restrictions in affected areas, including: Bayan‑Ölgii province: Altai, Deluun, Ölgii, Tsengel and Khovd districts (soums) Khovd province: Altai, Bulgan and Üyench

If you are in Mongolia: avoid travel to affected areas follow the instructions of local authorities, including quarantine measures monitor local media for updates, as restrictions may change quickly and other areas could be affected

sections.heading: Pre-travel vaccines and medications
added: Yellow Fever - Country Entry Requirements Yellow fever is a disease caused by a flavivirus from the bite of an infected mosquito.
Learn more: Measles Hepatitis B Hepatitis B is a risk in every destination.

changes.updateNote: New information about Typhoon Bavi ('Warnings and insurance' page).

sections.heading: Typhoon Bavi
added: Typhoon Bavi is expected to have its strongest impact on Taiwan from 10 July to 11 July.
The Central Weather Administration is forecasting heavy to torrential rain, strong winds, rough seas and possible transport disruptions.
The greatest rainfall risk is for central and northern Taiwan.
Flash flooding and landslides are possible, particularly in mountainous areas, while coastal areas may experience large waves.
Monitor official weather warnings, check transport schedules, and follow the advice of local authorities.

changes.description: Inhoud van het advies gewijzigd (zonder nieuwe brondatum).
changes.updateNote: Information about wildfires ('Warnings and insurance' page).

sections.heading: Wildfires
added: Wildfires can occur across France during the summer months.
The risk is highest in the south of the country, particularly along the Mediterranean coast, in Corsica, and in areas of the south-west during periods of hot, dry weather.
Check the French weather service’s fire alert map before travelling and monitor local authority advice during your stay.
Conditions can change quickly and access to forests, countryside areas and tourist sites may be restricted at short notice.

changes.updateNote: New information about recent cases of drug-assisted robbery and sexual assault (see ‘Drug assisted robbery and sexual assault’ on the ‘Safety and security’ page).

sections.heading: Drug assisted robbery and sexual assault
added: There have been reports of criminals in Trinidad and Tobago using drugs, deception and sexual solicitation, including via dating apps, to facilitate robbery and sexual assault.
Victims may be lured and drugged or beaten and robbed.
Incidents have occurred in bars, nightclubs, hotels and on city streets, sometimes involving groups working together.

You should: avoid accepting invitations from strangers or inviting them to your accommodation take care when using dating apps keep food and drinks under supervision plan travel routes in advance, particularly at night If drugged, seek local medical attention
